NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to Joel son of Pethuel:
text.Rotherham: The word of Yahweh, which came unto Joel, son of Pethuel.
text.KJV: The word of the Lord that came to Joel the son of Pethuel.
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord which came to Joel, the son of Pethuel.
text.DRB: The word of our Lord, that was made to Joel the son of Phatuel.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O elders, give ear, all inhabitants of the land! Has such a thing happened in your days, or in the days of your ancestors?
text.Rotherham: Hear this, ye elders, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land,—hath this ever happened in your days? or in the days of your fathers?
text.KJV: Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers?
text.NABRE: I. Announcement of Unprecedented Disaster Listen to this, you elders! Pay attention, all who dwell in the land! Has anything like this ever happened in your lifetime, or in the lifetime of your ancestors?
text.DRB: Hear this ye ancients, and harken with your ears all ye inhabitants of the land: if this hath been done in your days, or in the days of your fathers?

text.NRSV: Tell your children of it, and let your children tell their children, and their children another generation.
text.Rotherham: Concerning it, to your children, tell ye the story,—and your children, to their children, and their children, to the generation following:—
text.KJV: Tell ye your children of it, and let your children tell their children, and their children another generation.
text.NABRE: Report it to your children. Have your children report it to their children, and their children to the next generation.
text.DRB: Upon this tell you to your children, and your children to their children, and their children to another generation.

text.NRSV: What the cutting locust left, the swarming locust has eaten. What the swarming locust left, the hopping locust has eaten, and what the hopping locust left, the destroying locust has eaten.
text.Rotherham: That which was left by the creeping locust, hath the swarming locust eaten, and, that which was left by the swarming locust, hath the grass locust eaten; and, that which was left by the grass locust, hath the corn locust eaten.
text.KJV: That which the palmerworm hath left hath the locust eaten; and that which the locust hath left hath the cankerworm eaten; and that which the cankerworm hath left hath the caterpiller eaten.
text.NABRE: What the cutter left, the swarming locust has devoured; What the swarming locust left, the hopper has devoured; What the hopper left, the consuming locust has devoured.
text.DRB: The residue of the eruke hath the locust eaten, and the residue of the locust hath the bruke eaten, and the residue of the bruke hath the blast eaten.

text.NRSV: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep; and wail, all you wine-drinkers, over the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Awake, ye drunkards, and weep, and howl, all ye drinkers of wine,—over the new wine, because it hath been cut off from your mouth.
text.KJV: Awake, ye drunkards, and weep; and howl, all ye drinkers of wine, because of the new wine; for it is cut off from your mouth.
text.NABRE: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep; wail, all you wine drinkers, Over the new wine, taken away from your mouths.
text.DRB: Awake you that be drunk, and weep and howl all ye, that drink wine in sweetness: because it is perished from your mouth.

text.NRSV: For a nation has invaded my land, powerful and innumerable; its teeth are lions’ teeth, and it has the fangs of a lioness.
text.Rotherham: For, a nation, hath come up over my land, bold, and without number,—his teeth, are the teeth of a lion, and, the fangs of a lioness, hath he!
text.KJV: For a nation is come up upon my land, strong, and without number, whose teeth are the teeth of a lion, and he hath the cheek teeth of a great lion.
text.NABRE: For a nation invaded my land, powerful and past counting, With teeth like a lion’s, fangs like those of a lioness.
text.DRB: For a nation is ascended upon my land, strong & innumerable: his teeth as the teeth of a lion: & his cheek-teeth as of a lion's whelp.

text.NRSV: It has laid waste my vines, and splintered my fig trees; it has stripped off their bark and thrown it down; their branches have turned white.
text.Rotherham: He hath turned my vine to a waste, and my fig-tree to splinters,—he hath, barked it clean, and cast it down, bleached are its branches.
text.KJV: He hath laid my vine waste, and barked my fig tree: he hath made it clean bare, and cast it away; the branches thereof are made white.
text.NABRE: It has stripped bare my vines, splintered my fig tree, Shearing off its bark and throwing it away, until its branches turn white.
text.DRB: He hath laid my vineyard into a desert, and hath pilled off the bark of my fig-tree: stripping he hath spoiled it, and cast it forth: the boughs thereof are made white.

text.NRSV: Lament like a virgin dressed in sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
text.Rotherham: Wail thou, like a virgin girded with sackcloth, for the owner of her youth.
text.KJV: Lament like a virgin girded with sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
text.NABRE: Wail like a young woman dressed in sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
text.DRB: Mourn as a virgin girded with sack-cloth upon the husband of her youth.

text.NRSV: The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off from the house of the Lord. The priests mourn, the ministers of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cut off are the meal-offering and the drink-offering, from the house of Yahweh,—In grief are the priests, the attendants on Yahweh:—
text.KJV: The meat offering and the drink offering is cut off from the house of the Lord; the priests, the Lord’s ministers, mourn.
text.NABRE: Grain offering and libation are cut off from the house of the Lord; In mourning are the priests, the ministers of the Lord.
text.DRB: Sacrifice and libation is perished out of the house of our Lord: the priests our Lord's ministers mourned.

text.NRSV: The fields are devastated, the ground mourns; for the grain is destroyed, the wine dries up, the oil fails.
text.Rotherham: Laid waste is the field, in grief is the soil,—for laid waste is the corn, abashed is the new wine, languisheth the oil.
text.KJV: The field is wasted, the land mourneth; for the corn is wasted: the new wine is dried up, the oil languisheth.
text.NABRE: The field is devastated; the farmland mourns, Because the grain is devastated, the wine has dried up, the oil has failed.
text.DRB: The region is destroyed, the ground hath mourned: because the wheat is wasted, the wine is confounded, the oil hath languished.

text.NRSV: Be dismayed, you farmers, wail, you vinedressers, over the wheat and the barley; for the crops of the field are ruined.
text.Rotherham: Turn pale, ye husbandmen, howl, ye vinedressers, over the wheat, and over the barley,—for perished is the harvest of the field.
text.KJV: Be ye ashamed, O ye husbandmen; howl, O ye vinedressers, for the wheat and for the barley; because the harvest of the field is perished.
text.NABRE: Be appalled, you farmers! wail, you vinedressers, Over the wheat and the barley, because the harvest in the field is ruined.
text.DRB: The husbandmen are confounded, the dressers of vines have howled upon the wheat, and the barley, because the harvest of the field is perished.

text.NRSV: The vine withers, the fig tree droops. Pomegranate, palm, and apple—all the trees of the field are dried up; surely, joy withers away among the people.
text.Rotherham: The vine, is abashed, and, the fig-tree, languisheth,—pomegranate, palm also, and apple—all the trees of the field, have withered, Yea abashed is gladness, away from the sons of men.
text.KJV: The vine is dried up, and the fig tree languisheth; the pomegranate tree, the palm tree also, and the apple tree, even all the trees of the field, are withered: because joy is withered away from the sons of men.
text.NABRE: The vine has dried up, the fig tree has withered; The pomegranate, even the date palm and the apple— every tree in the field has dried up. Joy itself has dried up among the people.
text.DRB: The vineyard is confounded, & the fig-tree hath languished: the pomegranate, & the palm-tree, and the apple-tree, & all the trees of the field are withered: because joy is confounded from the children of men.

text.NRSV: Put on sackcloth and lament, you priests; wail, you ministers of the altar. Come, pass the night in sackcloth, you ministers of my God! Grain offering and drink offering are withheld from the house of your God.
text.Rotherham: Gird yourselves and beat the breast—ye priests, howl, ye attendants of the altar, go in and wrap yourselves for the night in sackcloth, ye attendants on my God,—for, withholden from the house of your God, are the meal-offering and the drink- offering.
text.KJV: Gird yourselves, and lament, ye priests: howl, ye ministers of the altar: come, lie all night in sackcloth, ye ministers of my God: for the meat offering and the drink offering is withholden from the house of your God.
text.NABRE: Cry Out to the Lord Gird yourselves and lament, you priests! wail, ministers of the altar! Come, spend the night in sackcloth, ministers of my God! For the grain offering and the libation are withheld from the house of your God.
text.DRB: Gird yourselves, & mourn ye priests, howl ye ministers of the altar: go in, lie in sack-cloth ye ministers of my God: because sacrifice and libation is perished out of the house of your God.

text.NRSV: Sanctify a fast, call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of the Lord your God, and cry out to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Hallow ye a fast, Call a solemn assembly, Gather, O elders, all the inhabitants of the land, unto the house of Yahweh your God,—and make ye outcry unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Sanctify ye a fast, call a solemn assembly, gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land into the house of the Lord your God, and cry unto the Lord,
text.NABRE: Proclaim a holy fast! Call an assembly! Gather the elders, all who dwell in the land, To the house of the Lord, your God, and cry out to the Lord!
text.DRB: Sacrifice ye a fast, call an assembly, gather together the ancients, all the inhabitants of the land into the house of your God: and cry ye to our Lord:

text.NRSV: Alas for the day! For the day of the Lord is near, and as destruction from the Almighty it comes.
text.Rotherham: Alas for the day! For near is the day of Yahweh, and, as a veritable Destruction from the Destroyer, shall it come.
text.KJV: Alas for the day! for the day of the Lord is at hand, and as a destruction from the Almighty shall it come.
text.NABRE: O! The day! For near is the day of the Lord, like destruction from the Almighty it is coming!
text.DRB: Aa a, for the day: because the day of our Lord is at hand, and as destruction from the mighty it shall come.

text.NRSV: Is not the food cut off before our eyes, joy and gladness from the house of our God?
text.Rotherham: Is it not, before our eyes, that, food, hath been cut off? From the house of our God, rejoicing and exultation.
text.KJV: Is not the meat cut off before our eyes, yea, joy and gladness from the house of our God?
text.NABRE: Before our very eyes has not food been cut off? And from the house of our God, joy and gladness?
text.DRB: Why, are not the victuals perished before your eyes, joy and exultation out of the house of our God?

text.NRSV: The seed shrivels under the clods, the storehouses are desolate; the granaries are ruined because the grain has failed.
text.Rotherham: Rotted hath the seed, under their clods, Laid waste are their stores, Thrown down are the garners,—Yea abashed is the corn.
text.KJV: The seed is rotten under their clods, the garners are laid desolate, the barns are broken down; for the corn is withered.
text.NABRE: The seed lies shriveled beneath clods of dirt; the storehouses are emptied. The granaries are broken down, for the grain is dried up.
text.DRB: The beasts are rotted in their dung, the barns are destroyed, the store-houses are dissipated: because the wheat is confounded.

text.NRSV: How the animals groan! The herds of cattle wander about because there is no pasture for them; even the flocks of sheep are dazed.
text.Rotherham: How do the beasts groan! Perplexed are the herds of oxen, because there is no pasture for them,—even, the flocks of sheep, are destroyed!
text.KJV: How do the beasts groan! the herds of cattle are perplexed, because they have no pasture; yea, the flocks of sheep are made desolate.
text.NABRE: How the animals groan! The herds of cattle are bewildered! Because they have no pasture, even the flocks of sheep are starving.
text.DRB: Why groan the beast, why lowed the flocks of the herd? Because there is no pasture for them: yea & the flocks of the cattle are perished.

text.NRSV: To you, O Lord, I cry. For fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and flames have burned all the trees of the field.
text.Rotherham: Unto thee, O Yahweh, will I cry,—for, a fire, hath consumed the pastures of the wilderness, and, a flame, hath set ablaze all the trees of the field.
text.KJV: O Lord, to thee will I cry: for the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and the flame hath burned all the trees of the field.
text.NABRE: To you, Lord, I cry! for fire has devoured the wilderness pastures, flame has scorched all the trees in the field.
text.DRB: To thee o Lord will I cry: because fire hath eaten the beautiful things of the desert, and the flame hath burnt all the trees of the region.

text.NRSV: Even the wild animals cry to you because the watercourses are dried up, and fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Even the beasts of the field, moan unto thee,—because dried up are the channels of water, and, a fire, hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness.
text.KJV: The beasts of the field cry also unto thee: for the rivers of waters are dried up, and the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Even the animals in the wild cry out to you; For the streams of water have run dry, and fire has devoured the wilderness pastures.
text.DRB: Yea and the beasts of the field, as a garden bed thirsting for a shower, have looked up to thee, because the fountains of waters are dried up, and fire hath devoured the beautiful things of the desert.

text.NRSV: Blow the trumpet in Zion; sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of the Lord is coming, it is near—
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn in Zion, sound an alarm in my holy mountain, let all the inhabitants of the land, tremble,—for coming is the day of Yahweh, for it is near!—
text.KJV: Blow ye the trumpet in Zion, and sound an alarm in my holy mountain: let all the inhabitants of the land tremble: for the day of the Lord cometh, for it is nigh at hand;
text.NABRE: II. The Day of the Lord Chapter 2 The Day Approaches Blow the horn in Zion, sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of the Lord is coming! Yes, it approaches,
text.DRB: Sound ye with the trumpet in Sion, howl in my holy mount, let all the inhabitants of the land be troubled: Because the day of our Lord cometh,

text.NRSV: a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness! Like blackness spread upon the mountains a great and powerful army comes; their like has never been from of old, nor will be again after them in ages to come.
text.Rotherham: A day of obscurity and deep gloom, a day of cloud, and thick darkness, as dusk, spread over the mountains,—a people, many and bold, like whom, hath not been from age-past times, and, after whom, shall not be again unto the years of generation after generation.
text.KJV: A day of darkness and of gloominess, a day of clouds and of thick darkness, as the morning spread upon the mountains: a great people and a strong; there hath not been ever the like, neither shall be any more after it, even to the years of many generations.
text.NABRE: a day of darkness and gloom, a day of thick clouds! Like dawn spreading over the mountains, a vast and mighty army! Nothing like it has ever happened in ages past, nor will the future hold anything like it, even to the most distant generations.
text.DRB: because the day of darkness, and of mist is near, the day of cloud, & whirlwind: as the morning spread upon the mountains much people & strong: the like to it hath not been from the beginning, and after it shall not be even unto the years of generation & generation.

text.NRSV: Fire devours in front of them, and behind them a flame burns. Before them the land is like the garden of Eden, but after them a desolate wilderness, and nothing escapes them.
text.Rotherham: Before him, hath a fire, devoured, and, after him, shall a flame, consume,—As the garden of Eden, is the land before him, but, after him, a desert most desolate, Moreover also, escape, giveth he none.
text.KJV: A fire devoureth before them; and behind them a flame burneth: the land is as the garden of Eden before them, and behind them a desolate wilderness; yea, and nothing shall escape them.
text.NABRE: Before it, fire devours, behind it flame scorches. The land before it is like the garden of Eden, and behind it, a desolate wilderness; from it nothing escapes.
text.DRB: Before the face thereof a devouring fire, & after it a burning flame: the land before it as it were a garden of pleasure, and after it the wilderness of a desert, neither is there that can escape it.

text.NRSV: They have the appearance of horses, and like war-horses they charge.
text.Rotherham: As the appearance of horses, is his appearance, and, as war-horses, so, shall they run:
text.KJV: The appearance of them is as the appearance of horses; and as horsemen, so shall they run.
text.NABRE: Their appearance is that of horses; like war horses they run.
text.DRB: As the appearance of horses, their appearance, & as horsemen so shall they run.

text.NRSV: As with the rumble of chariots, they leap on the tops of the mountains, like the crackling of a flame of fire devouring the stubble, like a powerful army drawn up for battle.
text.Rotherham: Like the noise of chariots on the tops of the mountains, shall they rattle along, like the noise of a flame of fire, devouring dry straw,—like a people bold, arrayed for battle.
text.KJV: Like the noise of chariots on the tops of mountains shall they leap, like the noise of a flame of fire that devoureth the stubble, as a strong people set in battle array.
text.NABRE: Like the rumble of chariots they hurtle across mountaintops; Like the crackling of fiery flames devouring stubble; Like a massive army in battle formation.
text.DRB: As the sound of chariots upon the tops of mountains shall they leap, as the sound of a flame of fire devouring stubble, as a strong people prepared to battle.

text.NRSV: Before them peoples are in anguish, all faces grow pale.
text.Rotherham: Because of him, shall peoples’, be in anguish,—all faces, have withdrawn their colour.
text.KJV: Before their face the people shall be much pained: all faces shall gather blackness.
text.NABRE: Before them peoples tremble, every face turns pale.
text.DRB: At his presence the people shall be vexed: all visages shall be made like a pot.

text.NRSV: Like warriors they charge, like soldiers they scale the wall. Each keeps to its own course, they do not swerve from their paths.
text.Rotherham: Like heroes, shall they run, like men of war, shall they mount a wall,—and, every one—along his own road, shall they march along, and shall not change their paths;
text.KJV: They shall run like mighty men; they shall climb the wall like men of war; and they shall march every one on his ways, and they shall not break their ranks:
text.NABRE: Like warriors they run, like soldiers they scale walls, Each advancing in line, without swerving from the course.
text.DRB: They shall run as valiants: as men of war they shall scale the wall: the men shall march in their ways, & shall not decline from their paths.

text.NRSV: They do not jostle one another, each keeps to its own track; they burst through the weapons and are not halted.
text.Rotherham: Nor, against each other, shall they strike, Each—on his own highway, shall they march,—though, in among the weapons, they fall, they shall not stop.
text.KJV: Neither shall one thrust another; they shall walk every one in his path: and when they fall upon the sword, they shall not be wounded.
text.NABRE: No one crowds the other; each advances in its own track; They plunge through the weapons; they are not checked.
text.DRB: None shall press his brother: they shall walk every one in their own path: yea & through the windows they shall fall, & shall not demolish.

text.NRSV: They rush upon the city, they run upon the wall; they climb up into the houses, they enter through the windows like a thief.
text.Rotherham: Upon the city, shall they leap, on the wall, shall they run, up the houses, shall they climb,—through the windows, shall they enter, like a thief,
text.KJV: They shall run to and fro in the city; they shall run upon the wall, they shall climb up upon the houses; they shall enter in at the windows like a thief.
text.NABRE: They charge the city, they run upon the wall, they climb into the houses; Through the windows they enter like thieves.
text.DRB: They shall enter the city: they shall run on the wall, they shall climb the houses, by the windows they shall enter as a thief.

text.NRSV: The earth quakes before them, the heavens tremble. The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.
text.Rotherham: Before him, hath quaked the earth, have trembled the heavens,—the sun and the moon, have become dark, and, the stars, have withdrawn their shining;
text.KJV: The earth shall quake before them; the heavens shall tremble: the sun and the moon shall be dark, and the stars shall withdraw their shining:
text.NABRE: Before them the earth trembles; the heavens shake; Sun and moon are darkened, and the stars withhold their brightness.
text.DRB: At his presence the earth hath trembled, the heavens are moved: the sun and moon are darkened, and the stars have withdrawn their shining.

text.NRSV: The Lord utters his voice at the head of his army; how vast is his host! Numberless are those who obey his command. Truly the day of the Lord is great; terrible indeed—who can endure it?
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, hath uttered his voice, before his host, for great indeed is his camp, for bold is he who executeth his word,—for great is the day of Yahweh, and awful exceedingly, Who then shall endure it?
text.KJV: And the Lord shall utter his voice before his army: for his camp is very great: for he is strong that executeth his word: for the day of the Lord is great and very terrible; and who can abide it?
text.NABRE: The Lord raises his voice at the head of his army; How immense is his host! How numerous those who carry out his command! How great is the day of the Lord! Utterly terrifying! Who can survive it?
text.DRB: And our Lord hath given his voice before the face of his host: because his camps are exceeding many, because strong & doing his word: for great is the day of our Lord, and terrible exceedingly: & who shall sustain it?

text.NRSV: Yet even now, says the Lord, return to me with all your heart, with fasting, with weeping, and with mourning;
text.Rotherham: Even now, therefore, urgeth Yahweh, Turn ye unto me, with all your heart,—and with fasting and with weeping, and with lamentation;
text.KJV: Therefore also now, saith the Lord, turn ye even to me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning:
text.NABRE: Return to the Lord Yet even now—oracle of the Lord — return to me with your whole heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning.
text.DRB: Now therefore saith our Lord: Convert to me in all your heart, in fasting, & in weeping, and in mourning.

text.NRSV: rend your hearts and not your clothing. Return to the Lord, your God, for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, and relents from punishing.
text.Rotherham: And rend your heart, and not your garments, turn therefore, unto Yahweh your God,—for, gracious and full of compassion, is he, slow to anger, and abundant in loving- kindness, and will grieve over calamity.
text.KJV: And rend your heart, and not your garments, and turn unto the Lord your God: for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repenteth him of the evil.
text.NABRE: Rend your hearts, not your garments, and return to the Lord, your God, For he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love, and relenting in punishment.
text.DRB: And rent your hearts, and not your garments, & turn to the Lord your God: because he is benign & merciful, patient and of much mercy, & ready to be gracious upon the malice.

text.NRSV: Who knows whether he will not turn and relent, and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for the Lord, your God?
text.Rotherham: Who knoweth, he may turn and grieve,—and leave behind him, a blessing, a meal-offering and a drink-offering, to Yahweh your God?
text.KJV: Who knoweth if he will return and repent, and leave a blessing behind him; even a meat offering and a drink offering unto the Lord your God?
text.NABRE: Perhaps he will again relent and leave behind a blessing, Grain offering and libation for the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: Who knoweth if he will convert, & forgive, and leave after him benediction, sacrifice, and libament to the Lord your God?

text.NRSV: Blow the trumpet in Zion; sanctify a fast; call a solemn assembly;
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn, in Zion,—hallow a fast, call a solemn assembly:
text.KJV: Blow the trumpet in Zion, sanctify a fast, call a solemn assembly:
text.NABRE: Blow the horn in Zion! Proclaim a fast, call an assembly!
text.DRB: Sound ye with the trumpet in Sion, sanctify a fast, call an assembly,

text.NRSV: gather the people. Sanctify the congregation; assemble the aged; gather the children, even infants at the breast. Let the bridegroom leave his room, and the bride her canopy.
text.Rotherham: Gather the people, hallow a convocation, collect the elders, gather the children, and the sucklings of the breasts,—let the bridegroom, come forth, from his chamber, and the bride from her bower:
text.KJV: Gather the people, sanctify the congregation, assemble the elders, gather the children, and those that suck the breasts: let the bridegroom go forth of his chamber, and the bride out of her closet.
text.NABRE: Gather the people, sanctify the congregation; Assemble the elderly; gather the children, even infants nursing at the breast; Let the bridegroom leave his room, and the bride her bridal tent.
text.DRB: gather together the people, sanctify the Church, assemble the ancients, gather together the little ones, & them that suck the breasts: let the bridegroom go forth to his chamber, & the bride out of her bride-chamber.

text.NRSV: Between the vestibule and the altar let the priests, the ministers of the Lord, weep. Let them say, “Spare your people, O Lord, and do not make your heritage a mockery, a byword among the nations. Why should it be said among the peoples, ‘Where is their God?’ ”
text.Rotherham: Between the porch and the altar, let the priests, weep, the attendants of Yahweh,—and let them say—Look with pity, O Yahweh, upon thy people, and do not deliver thine inheritance to reproach, that the nations, should mock them, Why should they say among the peoples, Where is their God?
text.KJV: Let the priests, the ministers of the Lord, weep between the porch and the altar, and let them say, Spare thy people, O Lord, and give not thine heritage to reproach, that the heathen should rule over them: wherefore should they say among the people, Where is their God?
text.NABRE: Between the porch and the altar let the priests weep, let the ministers of the Lord weep and say: “Spare your people, Lord! do not let your heritage become a disgrace, a byword among the nations! Why should they say among the peoples, ‘Where is their God?’”
text.DRB: Between the porch & the altar the priests our Lord's ministers shall weep, & shall say: Spare o Lord, spare thy people: and give not thine inheritance into reproach, that the nations have dominion over them. Why say they in the peoples: Where is their God?

text.NRSV: Then the Lord became jealous for his land, and had pity on his people.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh became jealous for his land,—and took pity on his people;
text.KJV: Then will the Lord be jealous for his land, and pity his people.
text.NABRE: The Lord Relents. Then the Lord grew jealous for his land and took pity on his people.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath been zealous to his land, & hath spared his people.

text.NRSV: In response to his people the Lord said: I am sending you grain, wine, and oil, and you will be satisfied; and I will no more make you a mockery among the nations.
text.Rotherham: Then answered Yahweh, and said to his people:—Behold me! sending you the corn, and the new wine and the oil, so shall ye be satisfied therewith; and I will not make you, any more, a reproach among the nations.
text.KJV: Yea, the Lord will answer and say unto his people, Behold, I will send you corn, and wine, and oil, and ye shall be satisfied therewith: and I will no more make you a reproach among the heathen:
text.NABRE: In response the Lord said to his people: I am sending you grain, new wine, and oil, and you will be satisfied by them; Never again will I make you a disgrace among the nations.
text.DRB: And our Lord answered, and said to his people: Behold I will send you corn, and wine, and oil, and you shall be replenished with them: & I will give you no more to be a reproach in the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: I will remove the northern army far from you, and drive it into a parched and desolate land, its front into the eastern sea, and its rear into the western sea; its stench and foul smell will rise up, for he has done great things.
text.Rotherham: And, the Northerner, will I remove far from you, and drive him into a land parched and desolate, with, his face, toward the eastern sea, and, his rear, toward the hinder sea,—then shall come up his ill odour, yea his stench, shall ascend, because he hath shown himself great in doing.
text.KJV: But I will remove far off from you the northern army, and will drive him into a land barren and desolate, with his face toward the east sea, and his hinder part toward the utmost sea, and his stink shall come up, and his ill savour shall come up, because he hath done great things.
text.NABRE: The northerner I will remove far from you, driving them out into a dry and desolate land, Their vanguard to the eastern sea, their rearguard to the western sea, And their stench will rise, their stink will ascend, What great deeds the Lord has done!
text.DRB: And him that is from the North, I will make far from you: & I will expel him into a land unpassable, & desert, his face against the east sea, & his extreme part to the last sea: & his stink shall ascend, & his rottenness shall ascend, because he hath done proudly.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, O soil; be glad and rejoice, for the Lord has done great things!
text.Rotherham: Be not thou afraid, O soil,—exult and rejoice, because Yahweh, hath shown himself great, in doing.
text.KJV: Fear not, O land; be glad and rejoice: for the Lord will do great things.
text.NABRE: Do not fear, O land! delight and rejoice, for the Lord has done great things!
text.DRB: Fear not o land, rejoice & be glad: because our Lord hath magnified to do.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, you animals of the field, for the pastures of the wilderness are green; the tree bears its fruit, the fig tree and vine give their full yield.
text.Rotherham: Be not afraid, ye beasts of the field, for sprouted have the pastures of the wilderness,—for, the tree, hath borne its fruit, the fig-tree and the vine, have yielded their wealth.
text.KJV: Be not afraid, ye beasts of the field: for the pastures of the wilderness do spring, for the tree beareth her fruit, the fig tree and the vine do yield their strength.
text.NABRE: Do not fear, you animals in the wild, for the wilderness pastures sprout green grass. The trees bear fruit, the fig tree and the vine produce their harvest.
text.DRB: Fear not ye beasts of the region: because the beautiful things of the desert are sprung, because the tree hath brought his fruit, the fig-tree, & the vine have given their vigour.

text.NRSV: O children of Zion, be glad and rejoice in the Lord your God; for he has given the early rain for your vindication, he has poured down for you abundant rain, the early and the latter rain, as before.
text.Rotherham: Ye sons of Zion, then, exult and be glad in Yahweh your God, for he hath given you the seed-rain, in right manner,—Yea he hath caused to descend for you a down-pour, of seed-rain and of the harvest-rain in the first month;
text.KJV: Be glad then, ye children of Zion, and rejoice in the Lord your God: for he hath given you the former rain moderately, and he will cause to come down for you the rain, the former rain, and the latter rain in the first month.
text.NABRE: Children of Zion, delight and rejoice in the Lord, your God! For he has faithfully given you the early rain, sending rain down on you, the early and the late rains as before.
text.DRB: And ye children of Sion rejoice, & be joyful in the Lord your God: because he hath given you a Doctor of justice, & he will make the early and the late shower to descend to you as in the beginning.

text.NRSV: The threshing floors shall be full of grain, the vats shall overflow with wine and oil.
text.Rotherham: So shall the threshing-floors, be filled, with corn,—and the vats, overflow, with new wine and oil.
text.KJV: And the floors shall be full of wheat, and the fats shall overflow with wine and oil.
text.NABRE: The threshing floors will be full of grain, the vats spilling over with new wine and oil.
text.DRB: And the floors shall be filled with wheat, and the presses shall overflow with wine and oil.

text.NRSV: I will repay you for the years that the swarming locust has eaten, the hopper, the destroyer, and the cutter, my great army, which I sent against you.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make good to you the years which were eaten by the swarming locust, the grass locust, and the corn locust and the creeping locust,—even my great army, which I sent among you.
text.KJV: And I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten, the cankerworm, and the caterpiller, and the palmerworm, my great army which I sent among you.
text.NABRE: I will repay you double what the swarming locust has eaten, The hopper, the consuming locust, and the cutter, my great army I sent against you.
text.DRB: And I will render you the years, which the locust, the bruke, and the blast, and the eruke hath eaten: my great strength which I have sent upon you.

text.NRSV: You shall eat in plenty and be satisfied, and praise the name of the Lord your God, who has dealt wondrously with you. And my people shall never again be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall eat and eat, and be satisfied, and shall praise the name of Yahweh your God, who hath dealt with you wondrously,—so shall my people, not be abashed, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of the Lord your God, that hath dealt wondrously with you: and my people shall never be ashamed.
text.NABRE: You will eat until you are fully satisfied, then you will praise the name of the Lord, your God, Who acts so wondrously on your behalf! My people will never again be put to shame.
text.DRB: And you shall eat eating, and shall be filled: and you shall praise the name of the Lord your God, that hath done marvels with you, and my people shall not be confounded for ever.

text.NRSV: You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I, the Lord, am your God and there is no other. And my people shall never again be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, in the midst of Israel, I am, and that, I, Yahweh, am your God, and none else,—and my people, shall not be abashed, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And ye shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I am the Lord your God, and none else: and my people shall never be ashamed.
text.NABRE: Then you will know that I am in the midst of Israel: I, the Lord, am your God, and there is no other; my people will never again be put to shame.
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am in the midst of Israel: & I the Lord your God, and there is none besides: and my people shall not be confounded for ever.

text.NRSV: Then afterward I will pour out my spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, afterwards, I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters, shall prophesy,—your old men, shall dream, dreams, your young men, shall see, visions;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions:
text.DRB: And it shall be after this: I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh: and your sons, and your daughters shall prophesy: your ancients shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions.

text.NRSV: Even on the male and female slaves, in those days, I will pour out my spirit.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, upon the servants and upon the handmaids—in those days, will I pour out my spirit;
text.KJV: And also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my spirit.
text.DRB: Yea and upon my servants, and handmaids in those days I will pour out my spirit.

text.NRSV: I will show portents in the heavens and on the earth, blood and fire and columns of smoke.
text.Rotherham: And I will set forth wonders in the heavens, and in the earth,—blood, and fire, and columns of smoke:
text.KJV: And I will shew wonders in the heavens and in the earth, blood, and fire, and pillars of smoke.
text.DRB: And I will give wonders in heaven, and in earth, blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke.

text.NRSV: The sun shall be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the great and terrible day of the Lord comes.
text.Rotherham: The sun, shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood,—before the coming of the great and awful day of Yahweh.
text.KJV: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and the terrible day of the Lord come.
text.DRB: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood: before the great and horrible day of the Lord doth come.

text.NRSV: Then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved; for in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be those who escape, as the Lord has said, and among the survivors shall be those whom the Lord calls.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, whosoever, shall call on the name of Yahweh, shall be delivered,—For in Mount Zion, and in Jerusalem, shall be a delivered remnant, just as Yahweh hath said, and among the survivors, whom Yahweh doth call.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be delivered: for in mount Zion and in Jerusalem shall be deliverance, as the Lord hath said, and in the remnant whom the Lord shall call.
text.DRB: And it shall be, every one that shall invocate the name of the Lord, shall be saved: because in mount Sion, and in Jerusalem shall be salvation, as our Lord hath said, and in the residue whom our Lord shall call.

text.NRSV: For then, in those days and at that time, when I restore the fortunes of Judah and Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: For lo! in those days, and at that time,—when I shall bring back the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,
text.KJV: For, behold, in those days, and in that time, when I shall bring again the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,
text.NABRE: III. The Lord’s Final Judgment Chapter 3 The Day of the Lord It shall come to pass I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, your young men will see visions.
text.DRB: Because lo in those days, and in that time when I shall convert the captivity of Juda, & Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the valley of Jehoshaphat, and I will enter into judgment with them there, on account of my people and my heritage Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations. They have divided my land,
text.Rotherham: Then will I gather all the nations, and bring them down into the Vale of Jehoshaphat,—and will enter into judgment with them there, concerning my people, and mine inheritance Israel, whom they scattered among the nations, and, my land, they apportioned;
text.KJV: I will also gather all nations, and will bring them down into the valley of Jehoshaphat, and will plead with them there for my people and for my heritage Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations, and parted my land.
text.NABRE: Even upon your male and female servants, in those days, I will pour out my spirit.
text.DRB: I will gather together all Nations, and will lead them into the valley of Josaphat: and I will plead with them there upon my people, and mine inheritance Israel, whom they have dispersed in the nations, and have divided my land.

text.NRSV: and cast lots for my people, and traded boys for prostitutes, and sold girls for wine, and drunk it.
text.Rotherham: And, for my people, they cast lots,—and gave a boy for a harlot, and a girl sold they for wine—and drank.
text.KJV: And they have cast lots for my people; and have given a boy for an harlot, and sold a girl for wine, that they might drink.
text.NABRE: I will set signs in the heavens and on the earth, blood, fire, and columns of smoke;
text.DRB: And upon my people they have cast lot: & boy they have given to be a strumpet, and wench they have sold for wine, that they might drink.

text.NRSV: What are you to me, O Tyre and Sidon, and all the regions of Philistia? Are you paying me back for something? If you are paying me back, I will turn your deeds back upon your own heads swiftly and speedily.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, what have, ye, to do with me, O Tyre and Zidon, and all the circuit of Palestine? A recompense, are ye paying back unto me? But, though ye should make a recompense unto me, swiftly, speedily, would I return your recompense upon your own head.
text.KJV: Yea, and what have ye to do with me, O Tyre, and Zidon, and all the coasts of Palestine? will ye render me a recompence? and if ye recompense me, swiftly and speedily will I return your recompence upon your own head;
text.NABRE: The sun will darken, the moon turn blood-red, Before the day of the Lord arrives, that great and terrible day.
text.DRB: But what is to me and to you o Tyre, and Sidon, and all the border of the Palesthines? What, will you render me revenge, and if you do revenge against me, I will soon render you quickly recompense upon your head.

text.NRSV: For you have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried my rich treasures into your temples.
text.Rotherham: Because, my silver and my gold, ye took away,—and, my richly beautiful things, carried ye into your temples;
text.KJV: Because ye have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried into your temples my goodly pleasant things:
text.NABRE: Then everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will escape harm. For on Mount Zion there will be a remnant, as the Lord has said, And in Jerusalem survivors whom the Lord will summon.
text.DRB: For my silver, and my gold you have taken: and my desirable things, and most beautiful you have carried into your temples.

text.NRSV: You have sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks, removing them far from their own border.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Judah and the sons of Jerusalem, ye sold to the sons of Greece,—that they might be far removed from their own boundary.
text.KJV: The children also of Judah and the children of Jerusalem have ye sold unto the Grecians, that ye might remove them far from their border.
text.DRB: And the children of Jerusalem you have sold to the children of the Greeks, that you might make them far off from their coasts.

text.NRSV: But now I will rouse them to leave the places to which you have sold them, and I will turn your deeds back upon your own heads.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! rousing them up out of the place whither ye sold them,—So will I bring back your dealing upon your own head.
text.KJV: Behold, I will raise them out of the place whither ye have sold them, and will return your recompence upon your own head:
text.DRB: Behold I will raise them up out of the place, wherein you have sold them: and I will turn your retribution upon your own head.

text.NRSV: I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the people of Judah, and they will sell them to the Sabeans, to a nation far away; for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the sons of Judah, and they will sell them to Sabeans, unto a nation afar off,—for, Yahweh, hath spoken.
text.KJV: And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah, and they shall sell them to the Sabeans, to a people far off: for the Lord hath spoken it.
text.DRB: And I will sell your sons, and your daughters into the hands of the children of Juda, and they shall sell them to the Sabæans, a nation far off, because our Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: Proclaim this among the nations: Prepare war, stir up the mighty; let all the warriors draw near, let them come up.
text.Rotherham: Proclaim ye this, among the nations, Hallow a war, rouse the mighty ones, let them draw near, come up, all the men of war!
text.KJV: Proclaim ye this among the Gentiles; Prepare war, wake up the mighty men, let all the men of war draw near; let them come up:
text.DRB: Proclaim ye this in the Gentiles: sanctify battle, raise up the strong: let them come, let all the men of war come up.

text.NRSV: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears; let the weak say, “I am a warrior.”
text.Rotherham: Beat your plough-shares into swords, and your pruning-hooks into spears,—as for the weak, let him say, mighty, I am.
text.KJV: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruninghooks into spears: let the weak say, I am strong.
text.DRB: Cut your ploughs into swords, and your spades into spears. Let the weak say: That I am strong.

text.NRSV: Hurry and come, all you nations from all around, come together there. Bring down your warriors, O Lord.
text.Rotherham: Give help—and come in, all ye nations on every side, and gather yourselves together,—Thither, bring down, O Yahweh, thy mighty ones!
text.KJV: Assemble yourselves, and come, all ye heathen, and gather yourselves together round about: thither cause thy mighty ones to come down, O Lord.
text.DRB: Break out, and come all ye nations from round about, and be gathered together: there will our Lord make all thy strong ones to be slain.

text.NRSV: Let the nations rouse themselves, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat; for there I will sit to judge all the neighboring nations.
text.Rotherham: Let the nations, be roused, and come up, into the Vale of Jehoshaphat,—for, there, will I sit to judge all the nations, on every side.
text.KJV: Let the heathen be wakened, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat: for there will I sit to judge all the heathen round about.
text.DRB: Let them arise, & let the Gentiles ascend into the valley of Josaphat: because there I will sit, to judge all nations round about.

text.NRSV: Put in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. Go in, tread, for the wine press is full. The vats overflow, for their wickedness is great.
text.Rotherham: Thrust ye in the vintage knife, for, grown ripe, is the vintage,—Go in, tread down, for full is the winepress, flow over do the vats, for abundant is their wickedness.
text.KJV: Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe: come, get you down; for the press is full, the fats overflow; for their wickedness is great.
text.DRB: Put in the sithes, because the harvest is ripe: come, and descend, because the press is full, the presses run over: because their malice is multiplied.

text.NRSV: Multitudes, multitudes, in the valley of decision! For the day of the Lord is near in the valley of decision.
text.Rotherham: Multitudes, multitudes, in the vale of strict decision,—for near is the day of Yahweh, in the vale of strict decision.
text.KJV: Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision: for the day of the Lord is near in the valley of decision.
text.DRB: Peoples peoples in the valley of concision, because the day of our Lord is nigh in the valley of concision.

text.NRSV: The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.
text.Rotherham: The sun and the moon, have become dark,—and, the stars, have withdrawn their shining,
text.KJV: The sun and the moon shall be darkened, and the stars shall withdraw their shining.
text.DRB: The sun and the moon are darkened, & the stars have withdrawn their shining.

text.NRSV: The Lord roars from Zion, and utters his voice from Jerusalem, and the heavens and the earth shake. But the Lord is a refuge for his people, a stronghold for the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, out of Zion, will roar, and, out of Jerusalem, will utter his voice, and the heavens and the earth, shall tremble,—but, Yahweh, shall be a shelter to his people, and a refuge to the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: The Lord also shall roar out of Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the heavens and the earth shall shake: but the Lord will be the hope of his people, and the strength of the children of Israel.
text.DRB: And our Lord will roar out of Sion, and out of Jerusalem he will give his voice: and the heavens, and the earth shall be moved, and our Lord the hope of his people, and the strength of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: So you shall know that I, the Lord your God, dwell in Zion, my holy mountain. And Jerusalem shall be holy, and strangers shall never again pass through it.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, Yahweh, am your God, making my habitation in Zion my holy mountain,—So shall, Jerusalem, be, holy, and, foreigners, shall pass through her no more.
text.KJV: So shall ye know that I am the Lord your God dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain: then shall Jerusalem be holy, and there shall no strangers pass through her any more.
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am the Lord your God dwelling in Sion my holy mount: and Jerusalem shall be holy, and strangers shall pass through it no more.

text.NRSV: In that day the mountains shall drip sweet wine, the hills shall flow with milk, and all the stream beds of Judah shall flow with water; a fountain shall come forth from the house of the Lord and water the Wadi Shittim.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow down with milk, and, all the channels of Judah, shall flow down with waters,—and, a spring, out of the house of Yahweh, shall come forth, and shall water the torrent-valley of the acacias.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the mountains shall drop down new wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the rivers of Judah shall flow with waters, and a fountain shall come forth of the house of the Lord, and shall water the valley of Shittim.
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day: the mountains shall distill sweetness, and the hills shall flow with milk: and through all the rivers of Juda shall run waters: and a fountain shall issue out of the house of the Lord, and shall water the torrent of thorns.

text.NRSV: Egypt shall become a desolation and Edom a desolate wilderness, because of the violence done to the people of Judah, in whose land they have shed innocent blood.
text.Rotherham: Egypt, to a desolation, shall be turned, and, Edom, to a desert most desolate, shall be changed,—because of the violence done to the sons of Judah, in that they shed innocent blood in their land.
text.KJV: Egypt shall be a desolation, and Edom shall be a desolate wilderness, for the violence against the children of Judah, because they have shed innocent blood in their land.
text.DRB: Ægypt shall be into desolation, & Idumea into a desert of perdition, for that they have done unjustly against the children of Juda, and have shed innocent blood in their land.

text.NRSV: But Judah shall be inhabited forever, and Jerusalem to all generations.
text.Rotherham: But, Judah, age-abidingly, shall remain, and, Jerusalem, to generation after generation.
text.KJV: But Judah shall dwell for ever, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.
text.DRB: And Jewry shall be inhabited for ever, and Jerusalem unto generation and generation.

text.NRSV: I will avenge their blood, and I will not clear the guilty, for the Lord dwells in Zion.
text.Rotherham: And I will free from their blood-guiltiness them whom I had not freed,—for, Yahweh, is about to make his habitation in Zion.
text.KJV: For I will cleanse their blood that I have not cleansed: for the Lord dwelleth in Zion.
text.DRB: And I will cleanse their blood, which I had not cleansed: and the Lord will dwell in Sion.

2.28: III. The Lord’s Final Judgment Chapter 3 The Day of the Lord It shall come to pass I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, your young men will see visions.
2.29: Even upon your male and female servants, in those days, I will pour out my spirit.
2.30: I will set signs in the heavens and on the earth, blood, fire, and columns of smoke;
2.31: The sun will darken, the moon turn blood-red, Before the day of the Lord arrives, that great and terrible day.
2.32: Then everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will escape harm. For on Mount Zion there will be a remnant, as the Lord has said, And in Jerusalem survivors whom the Lord will summon.

3.6: You sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks, taking them far from their own country!
3.7: Look! I am rousing them from the place to which you sold them, and I will turn your deeds back upon your own head.
3.8: I will sell your sons and daughters to the Judahites who will sell them to the Sabeans, a distant nation. The Lord has spoken!
3.9: The Nations Destroyed Announce this to the nations: Proclaim a holy war! Alert the warriors! Let all the soldiers report and march!
3.10: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning knives into spears; let the weakling boast, “I am a warrior!”
3.11: Hurry and come, all you neighboring peoples, assemble there! Bring down, Lord, your warriors!
3.12: Let the nations rouse themselves and come up to the Valley of Jehoshaphat; For there I will sit in judgment upon all the neighboring nations.
3.13: Wield the sickle, for the harvest is ripe; Come and tread, for the wine press is full; The vats overflow, for their crimes are numerous.
3.14: Crowds upon crowds in the Valley of Decision; For near is the day of the Lord in the Valley of Decision.
3.15: Sun and moon are darkened, and the stars withhold their brightness,
3.16: The Lord roars from Zion, and from Jerusalem raises his voice, The heavens and the earth quake, but the Lord will be a shelter for his people, a fortress for the people of Israel.
3.17: A Secure Future for Judah Then you will know that I the Lord am your God, dwelling on Zion, my holy mountain; Jerusalem will be holy, and strangers will never again travel through her.
3.18: On that day the mountains will drip new wine, and the hills flow with milk, All the streams of Judah will flow with water. A spring will rise from the house of the Lord, watering the Valley of Shittim.
3.19: Egypt will be a waste, Edom a desolate wilderness, Because of violence done to the Judahites, because they shed innocent blood in their land.
3.20: But Judah will be inhabited forever, and Jerusalem for all generations.
3.21: I will avenge their blood, and I will not acquit the guilt. The Lord dwells in Zion.
